name: Seychelles
avoid: December–February is the wetter northwest monsoon, so Curieuse boat landings, mountain trails, and beach days are more likely to be interrupted by showers or rough seas.
July–August southeast trade winds can make Cat Cocos sailings choppier and some west-facing swimming beaches less comfortable even though temperatures stay pleasant.
Sunday is noticeably slower for buses, shops, and some casual restaurants outside resort areas, so avoid treating it as your main logistics day.
stopoverNote: For trips shorter than a week, skip Silhouette Island and split your stay between Mahé and a Praslin–La Digue combo instead of changing bases too often.

cities.name: Victoria (Mahé)
cities.notes: Use Mahé's capital as the practical arrival base for Sir Selwyn Selwyn-Clarke Market, Morne Seychellois hikes, Beau Vallon sunsets, and the easiest bus connections in the country.

cities.name: Praslin
cities.notes: Praslin is the classic second stop for Vallée de Mai, Curieuse day trips, Côte d'Or stays, and the easiest access to Anse Lazio.

cities.name: La Digue
cities.notes: La Digue is the slowest and most photogenic inner island, best explored by bicycle between L'Union Estate, Anse Source d'Argent, Grand Anse, and Anse Cocos.

cities.name: Silhouette Island
cities.notes: Silhouette is the quiet nature-and-resort finale for marine park snorkeling, rainforest trails, and a genuinely low-density island stay.

itinerary.sim: Cable & Wireless Seychelles or Airtel Seychelles prepaid SIMs are the simplest airport pickups; coverage is strongest on Mahé and Praslin, with smaller-island speeds more variable.

Victoria (Mahé).name: Victoria (Mahé)
Victoria (Mahé).note: Keep Mahé at the front of the trip for arrival recovery, capital sights, and your Morne Seychellois hiking window before moving to the outer inner islands.

days.theme: Victoria Market, Botanical Garden and a Gentle First Sunset on Mahé

activities.name: SPTC local bus rides around Victoria and Beau Vallon
activities.tip: The standard public bus fare is 12 SCR, so keep short hops on Mahé flexible instead of paying taxi rates for every transfer.

activities.name: Sir Selwyn Selwyn-Clarke Market
Victoria Clocktower and Independence Avenue walk
Seychelles National Botanical Gardens
Beau Vallon beach swim and sunset

days.theme: Morne Seychellois Hike, Mission Lodge and a North Coast Dinner

activities.name: Morne Blanc Trail in Morne Seychellois National Park
activities.tip: There is no park entrance fee, but start early because cloud build-up and midday humidity reduce the view quality fast.

activities.name: Mission Lodge viewpoint stop
Tea Factory lookout drive on Sans Souci Road
Port Launay Marine National Park snorkeling or kayak session

Praslin.name: Praslin
Praslin.note: Praslin is the best base for the two ticketed nature anchors—Vallée de Mai and Curieuse—while still leaving you room for one classic beach afternoon.

days.theme: Transfer to Praslin, Vallée de Mai and Côte d'Or Evening

activities.name: Cat Cocos ferry from Mahé to Praslin
activities.tip: Economy seats are the best-value choice on the Mahe–Praslin run; if the sea forecast looks rough, Air Seychelles is the smoother fallback.

activities.name: Air Seychelles domestic flight from Mahé to Praslin
Vallée de Mai Nature Reserve
activities.tip: Adult visitor tickets are 450 SCR, and the coco de mer explanations are far better if you join one of the scheduled ranger-guided walks.

activities.name: Côte d'Or beach swim and sunset

hotels.name: Coco de Mer Hotel and Black Parrot Suites

days.theme: Curieuse Island, Anse Lazio and Slow Praslin Logistics

activities.name: Curieuse Island landing fee
activities.tip: The Seychelles Parks fee is 300 SCR and is commonly collected as part of boat excursions from Anse Volbert or Baie Sainte Anne.

activities.name: Shared Curieuse and St Pierre boat excursion from Praslin
SPTC local bus ride between Baie Sainte Anne, Vallée de Mai and Anse Lazio
Anse Lazio beach stop

La Digue.name: La Digue
La Digue.note: La Digue deserves a full bicycle-paced block because the island works best when you are not rushing the beach, estate, and east-coast hiking rhythm.

days.theme: Cycle Arrival, L'Union Estate and Anse Source d'Argent

activities.name: Inter-island ferry from Praslin to La Digue
activities.tip: The crossing is short, so it is usually easier to keep luggage light and pick up your bicycle once you reach La Passe jetty.

activities.name: Bicycle rental on La Digue
activities.tip: A standard pedal bike usually costs 150–180 SCR a day and remains the default way to get around the island.

activities.name: L'Union Estate and Anse Source d'Argent access
activities.tip: The estate entrance fee is 150 SCR and covers the vanilla house, plantation grounds, giant tortoises, and repeated access to Anse Source d'Argent on the same day.

activities.name: La Passe village and old jetty sunset ride

days.theme: Grand Anse, Anse Cocos Hike and a Final Car-Free Day

activities.name: Grand Anse and Petite Anse beach circuit by bicycle
Anse Cocos hike
activities.tip: Carry water and start before the sun gets harsh because the trail has little shade in the open sections near Grand Anse.

activities.name: Veuve Nature Reserve stop
Clear-bottom kayak or snorkeling session from Anse Severe

Silhouette Island.name: Silhouette Island
Silhouette Island.note: Treat Silhouette as the quiet splurge or nature closer once you are done with the more logistically active Praslin–La Digue segment.

days.theme: Silhouette Boat Transfer, Marine Park Time and a Low-Key Final Night

activities.name: La Digue to Mahé return ferry plus Silhouette resort boat transfer
activities.tip: There is no regular public ferry to Silhouette, so most visitors route back via Mahé and use the scheduled Labriz or lodge boat.

activities.name: Silhouette Marine National Park snorkeling
Grand Barbe coastal trail or guided nature walk
Hilton Labriz jetty and beach sunset

connections.method: Taxi is fastest; SPTC airport-area buses are far cheaper if you are packing light and heading toward Victoria or Beau Vallon.
Best-value transfer is the Cat Cocos ferry from Mahé to Praslin; Air Seychelles is quicker and smoother in rougher sea conditions.
Short passenger ferry from Baie Sainte Anne to La Passe; keep this as a light-luggage hop rather than a full travel day.
Return by ferry via Praslin and Mahé, then connect to the scheduled resort or lodge boat because Silhouette has no open public ferry network.
Boat back to Mahé followed by a road transfer to SEZ; confirm the morning boat timing before choosing a same-day international departure.

extras: Indian passport holders do not need to arrange a visa in advance, but Seychelles still expects a valid passport, onward ticket, accommodation proof, and sufficient funds for the visitor's permit on arrival.
The 1 SCR ≈ ₹6 shortcut works well for quick mental math on buses, estate tickets, and takeaway meals, but larger hotels and tours sometimes quote directly in EUR or USD.
Mahé and Praslin buses are useful only with compact luggage because big suitcases are commonly refused on board, so save taxis for airport and pier-heavy transfer days.
Cat Cocos crossings can feel rough in trade-wind months, so motion-sickness tablets are worth carrying if you are choosing the ferry over Air Seychelles.
La Digue is built around cycling rather than cars, so sandals, sunscreen, and a small day bag are more useful than packing for heavy road-transfer logistics.

Victoria (Mahé): Sir Selwyn Selwyn-Clarke Market Victoria Seychelles
La Digue: Anse Source d'Argent La Digue Seychelles
